doc:
Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_clear_divby0 (mpfr/src/exceptions.c L168-L174). Three-line C body: `__gmpfr_flags &= MPFR_FLAGS_ALL ^ MPFR_FLAGS_DIVBY0` -- clear the DIVBY0 bit (bit 5 = 32) from the global flag register. C returns void.

The TS port follows the same immutable-API contract used by the rest of the flag family (see mpfr_divby0_p, mpfr_nanflag_p): it takes a `mask: bigint` describing the pre-clear flag state, applies the clear-DIVBY0 operation, and returns the new flag state as a `bigint`. This makes the port pure/stateless on its surface (the wire form encodes the global flag state explicitly), while still exercising the SAME `flags &= ALL ^ BIT` arithmetic the C performs.

The in-library wiring (when ported into src/) routes through clearFlags(MPFR_FLAGS_DIVBY0) from src/internal/mpfr/flags.ts -- already shipped at composite=1.0. The reference port reads that delegate; the production src/ops/clear_divby0.ts will too.

Class 'misc' (1000ms per-case budget). Tag minimums per Rule 7 apply -- the input domain (mask in [0, 63]) is small but enumerable. happy/edge/adversarial/fuzz coverage is built on the same six-flag truth-table the mpfr_divby0_p golden uses, so the same coverage shape applies.
divergence_from_c:
Three divergences:

  1. Return type: C returns void (mutates global state); TS returns `bigint` -- the new flag state after the clear operation. The immutable-API mandate (decision_api_shape memory) forbids global mutable state in the public surface.

  2. Parameter list: C takes no parameters (operates on the global __gmpfr_flags register); TS takes `mask: bigint` -- the desired pre-clear flag state. Matches the convention used by mpfr_divby0_p and the rest of the flag family.

  3. Mechanism: The port must either (a) use src/internal/mpfr/flags.ts directly (clearFlags(MPFR_FLAGS_DIVBY0)) or (b) compute the result inline as `mask & (MPFR_FLAGS_ALL ^ MPFR_FLAGS_DIVBY0)` = `mask & (63n ^ 32n)` = `mask & 31n`. Both are equivalent. Option (a) ties the port into the shared substrate; option (b) is self-contained. The reference port uses option (b) for simplicity; the production port will likely use option (a) for consistency with the rest of the flag family.
refs: mpfr/src/exceptions.c L168-L174 -- the C reference body.
src/internal/mpfr/flags.ts -- the TS flag register module (already shipped); clearFlags(MPFR_FLAGS_DIVBY0) is the canonical clear path.
src/ops/divby0_p.ts -- sister predicate that reads the same bit; uses the same mask-based wire form.
mpfr/tests/texceptions.c L327-L351 -- mined source pattern (clear, set_X, verify).
